title: Single-phase gradient-solvation-electrolyte-stabilized Li metal batteries
abstract: Published online: 08 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10732-z A targeted ligand anti-solvent is incorporated into an anion-rich ether-based electrolyte and used to achieve <mark>lithium</mark> metal batteries with a long cycle life, high <mark>energy density</mark> and high capacity retention.

title: Unravelling the key factors governing O<sub>2</sub> evolution upon charging a reversible LiOH-based nonaqueous Li | |O<sub>2</sub> battery
abstract: Published online: 09 July 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-75284-2 LiOH-based Li | |O2 batteries offer high <mark>energy density</mark> but suffer from poor reversibility. Here, authors study side-reaction origins and key reactive oxygen species during LiOH decomposition upon charge, and propose holistic strategies to effectively improve the Faradaic efficiency.
